title: Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple
tagline: Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Sree Kurumba Bhagavati Temple (alternatively Sree Kurumba Bhagavati Temple) is a Hindu temple at Kodungallur, Thrissur District, Kerala state, India.
overview.description: Sree Kurumba Bhagavati Temple (alternatively Sree Kurumba Bhagavati Temple) is a Hindu temple at Kodungallur, Thrissur District, Kerala state, India. It is dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali, a form of Mahakali worshipped and significantly revered in Kerala. The goddess is known also by the names "Sri Kurumba" and Kodungallur Amma (The Mother of Kodungallur). This temple is the head of 64 Bhadrakali kavus in Malabar.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Raviswarapuram Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Raviswarapuram Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva at Kodungalloor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva in Raviswara form, located in main Sanctum Sanctorum, facing East.

topPlaces.name: Kodungallur
topPlaces.description: Kodungallur (Malayalam: [koɖuŋːɐlːuːr]; formerly also known as Cranganore (anglicised name), Cranganor (Portuguese), Mahodayapuram, Shingly, Vanchi and Muziris/Muchiri/Muyirikkode) is a historically significant town located on the banks of the river Periyar on the Malabar Coast in Thrissur district of Kerala, India. It is 36 kilometres (22 mi) north of Kochi (Cochin) by National Highway 66 and 38 km (24 mi) from Thrissur.

topPlaces.name: Cheraman Juma Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Cheraman Juma Mosque is a mosque in Kodungallur, Thrissur district, in the state of Kerala, India. The mosque was built in 629 CE by Malik Bin Dinar though some scholars suggest a later construction date in the 14th–15th century.

topPlaces.name: Sringapuram Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Sringapuram Mahadeva Temple is a Shiva Temple, (Malayalam: ശൃംഗപുരം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) and is located at Kodungallur in Thrissur District of Kerala. The temple adjoins the Kodungallur Kovilakam Palace.

topPlaces.name: Puthen Kovilakam
topPlaces.description: Puthen Kovilakam (10°12′59″N 76°12′06″E) is one section of the Kodungallur Kovilakam, a palace in Kodungallur, Kerala, India. Puthen Kovilakam means "new palace".

topPlaces.name: Thiruvanchikulam Temple
topPlaces.description: Thiruvanchikulam Siva Temple (medieval Thiruvanchaikkalam Temple) is a Hindu temple situated in Kodungallur in Thrissur district of Kerala state, India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Chera period.

topPlaces.name: Keezhtali Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Keezhtali Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in Kodungalloor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India; it was one of the important temples in Chera Kingdom, being one of a complex of 108. According to folklore, sage Parashurama himself installed the idol of Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Methala
topPlaces.description: Methala is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala. Methala was added to Kodungallur Municipality on 11 November 2009.

items.note: Raviswarapuram Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva at Kodungalloor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva in Raviswara form, located in main Sanctum Sanctorum, facing East.

Kodungallur (Malayalam: [koɖuŋːɐlːuːr]; formerly also known as Cranganore (anglicised name), Cranganor (Portuguese), Mahodayapuram, Shingly, Vanchi and Muziris/Muchiri/Muyirikkode) is a historically significant town located on the banks of the river Periyar on the Malabar Coast in Thrissur district of Kerala, India. It is 36 kilometres (22 mi) north of Kochi (Cochin) by National Highway 66 and 38 km (24 mi) from Thrissur.

The Cheraman Juma Mosque is a mosque in Kodungallur, Thrissur district, in the state of Kerala, India. The mosque was built in 629 CE by Malik Bin Dinar though some scholars suggest a later construction date in the 14th–15th century.
Sringapuram Mahadeva Temple is a Shiva Temple, (Malayalam: ശൃംഗപുരം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) and is located at Kodungallur in Thrissur District of Kerala. The temple adjoins the Kodungallur Kovilakam Palace.
Puthen Kovilakam (10°12′59″N 76°12′06″E) is one section of the Kodungallur Kovilakam, a palace in Kodungallur, Kerala, India. Puthen Kovilakam means "new palace".

Thiruvanchikulam Siva Temple (medieval Thiruvanchaikkalam Temple) is a Hindu temple situated in Kodungallur in Thrissur district of Kerala state, India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Chera period.

Keezhtali Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in Kodungalloor of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India; it was one of the important temples in Chera Kingdom, being one of a complex of 108. According to folklore, sage Parashurama himself installed the idol of Shiva.
Methala is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala. Methala was added to Kodungallur Municipality on 11 November 2009.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kodungallur Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Raviswarapuram Siva Temple, Kodungallur, Cheraman Juma Mosque, Sringapuram Mahadeva Temple, Puthen Kovilakam.
